data.narrative: The student demonstrated a solid understanding of context engineering by analyzing the existing Express project, selecting a surgical context strategy, identifying a convention mismatch, and tracing it back to missing architectural context. They showed good engineering judgment by improving the context instead of changing the prompt.

dimension_scores.observation: Selected relevant project context and explained why each convention mattered.
dimension_scores.evidence: Analyzed server.js, package.json, routing, validation, and error handling.

dimension_scores.observation: Identified convention drift in API error handling.
dimension_scores.evidence: Compared existing JSON error responses against a hypothetical inconsistent format.

dimension_scores.observation: Improved context instead of rewriting prompts.
dimension_scores.evidence: Added architectural conventions and explicit constraints.

dimension_scores.observation: Applied context engineering principles to explain the root cause.
dimension_scores.evidence: Connected the convention mismatch directly to missing context.

dimension_scores.observation: Produced a consistent context-engineering workflow grounded in the existing project.
dimension_scores.evidence: Verified routing, validation, middleware, and response conventions.

strengths: Consistently connected architectural context to implementation quality.
data.recommendation: Continue documenting reusable architectural conventions before delegating implementation tasks to AI.

public_evidence.student_action: Reviewed project conventions and refined the context package.
public_evidence.technical_detail: server.js routing, validation, middleware, and error-response conventions.
public_evidence.proof: Inspected package.json and server.js while completing Module 3.
public_evidence.reviewer_value: Shows the ability to maintain consistency in AI-assisted development.

concepts_demonstrated.evidence: Explained architectural, local, and constraint context.

teaching_approaches.detail: Used the Express project to identify conventions.

learning_style_signals: Learns effectively from concrete project examples.
